mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 6.0 percent, labor market deterioration triggers recession fears that compress equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent across a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and residential housing starts signals a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate earnings momentum, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 40 percent of observed instances over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid policy tightening, rising short-term rates amplify the negative impact of cooling consumer credit on equity valuations, causing S&P 500 returns to fall sharply over the next week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25 percentage points in a single month
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns do not exhibit a statistically significant negative correlation with RSAFS declines when FEDFUNDS increases exceed 0.25pp in a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real gross domestic product expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically supporting positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain flat or negative despite consecutive quarters of positive GDPC1 growth exceeding 2.0 percent annualized rate over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 105.0, supply chain bottlenecks and capacity constraints reverse, triggering a disinflationary supply shock that boosts equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns fail to rise when INDPRO breaches the 105.0 threshold in more than 50 percent of instances across a 3-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising unemployment and elevated federal funds rates creates a dual pressure on corporate borrowing costs and consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The joint increase in UNRATE and FEDFUNDS does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 45 percent of observed instances over a 4-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where housing starts peak and consumer credit growth decelerates simultaneously, equity risk premiums expand rapidly, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST peaks and RSAFS declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive or statistically neutral when HOUST and RSAFS exhibit the specified peak-decline pattern across a 5-year historical sample.
